ticket.subject: Re: Reminder: FedEx REST API Migration for PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label App
ticket.raw_subject: Re: Reminder: FedEx REST API Migration for PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label App
ticket.description:
Hi PluginHive Support Team,

Shopify Store URL: dermahealer.co.uk
FedEx Account Number: 874939153

I am writing to get assistance regarding our FedEx migration from the
legacy SOAP API to the new REST API inside your Shopify app for
dermahealer.co.uk

I have strictly followed your official troubleshooting instructions,
but I am still stuck. I click the register button immediately after
entering my details, and the app displays a "Registration Failed"
error before proceeding any further.

Here is exactly what I have already done to troubleshoot:

Disabled Legacy Account: I disabled our old FedEx SOAP account inside
the app configuration to prevent conflicts.
Matched FedEx Profile Data: I verified and entered our company name
and address details exactly as they appear under the "FedEx Contact
Information" section in our portal.
Checked Billing Data: I also cross-referenced and tried the exact
formatting listed under "FedEx Accounts & Payments" to ensure zero
character mismatches.
Checked Formatting: I confirmed there are no extra trailing spaces and
that the text fits standard API limitations.

Despite these steps, the validation handshake continues to fail at the
exact same step.

Could you please check your backend logs for our Shopify store to see
the exact error response being returned by the FedEx REST gateway? P
lease let me know how we can get this bypassed or manually authenticated.

Thank you, and I look forward to your prompt response.

Best regards,
Arunas Druktenis


On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 10:22 AM Manvi <manvimeshram@pluginhive.com> wrote:
>
> Hi Arunas,
>
> I wanted to send a quick reminder regarding the FedEx Web Services Retirement that began on April 1, 2026. While the migration process is already underway, we have secured an extension for our customers until June 1, 2026.
>
> If your Shopify app — “PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label” — has not yet been migrated to FedEx REST APIs, your FedEx shipping rates, label generation, and tracking services may be impacted once the transition is fully enforced.
>
> Steps to Complete the Migration:
>
> Open your Shopify app settings
> Complete the FedEx REST API registration using your FedEx account details
> Verify your account via SMS, email, phone, invoice, or Help Desk
> Your new credentials will be configured automatically
> Run a quick test to confirm everything is working
>
> Please refer to the YouTube Tutorial walk through for step-by-step guidance during the migration process.
>
> If you encounter any issues or need assistance at any stage, simply reply to this email or contact us at support@pluginhive.com
>
> Best regards,
> Manvi M | Partnerships Executive
> The PluginHive Team
> FedEx Certified Compatible Solution Provider
>
> If you have already completed the migration, please ignore this email.

Hi PluginHive Support Team,

Shopify Store URL: dermahealer.co.uk
FedEx Account Number: 874939153

I am writing to get assistance regarding our FedEx migration from the
legacy SOAP API to the new REST API inside your Shopify app for
dermahealer.co.uk

I have strictly followed your official troubleshooting instructions,
but I am still stuck. I click the register button immediately after
entering my details, and the app displays a "Registration Failed"
error before proceeding any further.

Here is exactly what I have already done to troubleshoot:

Disabled Legacy Account: I disabled our old FedEx SOAP account inside
the app configuration to prevent conflicts.
Matched FedEx Profile Data: I verified and entered our company name
and address details exactly as they appear under the "FedEx Contact
Information" section in our portal.
Checked Billing Data: I also cross-referenced and tried the exact
formatting listed under "FedEx Accounts & Payments" to ensure zero
character mismatches.
Checked Formatting: I confirmed there are no extra trailing spaces and
that the text fits standard API limitations.

Despite these steps, the validation handshake continues to fail at the
exact same step.

Could you please check your backend logs for our Shopify store to see
the exact error response being returned by the FedEx REST gateway? P
lease let me know how we can get this bypassed or manually authenticated.

Thank you, and I look forward to your prompt response.

Best regards,
Arunas Druktenis

comments.body:
Hello Arunas,

Thank you for reaching out with your query.

The Registration will not go through if you had made 3 unsuccessful attempts as FedEx API will block new registration attempts.

Please wait for 24 hours from the time of the last attempt and try registering again.

Keep us informed of the progress so that we can assist you as needed.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Arunas,
 
Thank you for reaching out with your query.
 
The Registration will not go through if you had made 3 unsuccessful attempts as FedEx API will block new registration attempts.
 
Please wait for 24 hours from the time of the last attempt and try registering again.
 
Keep us informed of the progress so that we can assist you as needed.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Arunas,

Thank you for your response.

I noticed 2 things on your store when I tried adding the FedEx REST Account from my end -
- The Invoice you have shared is a VAT Invoice and not a shipping Invoice. Please share a shipping Invoice with us.
- You are subscribed to the Basic Plan with the app on both of your stores, which offers only 1 carrier integration. Could you please confirm if you have recently changed the subscription plan in the app and subscribed to Basic Plan? As you are on Basic Plan now, we are not able to add additional accounts in the app on your store.


Awaiting your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Arunas,
 
Thank you for your response.
 
I noticed 2 things on your store when I tried adding the FedEx REST Account from my end -
  • The Invoice you have shared is a VAT Invoice and not a shipping Invoice. Please share a shipping Invoice with us.
  • You are subscribed to the Basic Plan with the app on both of your stores, which offers only 1 carrier integration. Could you please confirm if you have recently changed the subscription plan in the app and subscribed to Basic Plan? As you are on Basic Plan now, we are not able to add additional accounts in the app on your store.
 
Awaiting your response.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

We have never experienced any issues using 2 carriers.



Thanks

Arunas

L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1778847898801819

Lithuania Customer using MCSL-S on 2 stores dermahealer-com.myshopify.com and dermahealer-uk.myshopify.com
Customer is active from 2021 in one store and 2023 on another. He is on Basic Plan on both stores.
Customer is using 3 accounts (2 active and 1 inactive) on both stores without any difficulties.
Customer is now trying to add the FedEx Account using REST in the app but the integration is not going through due to plan limitation.
We have verified that the details being provided are correct.
Could you please enable the app on customer store with another integration slot, so that we can add the FedEx REST Account without disturbing the current setup?

Hello Arunas,

Thank you for your patience.

We have added the FedEx REST Account in both your stores and updated the automation rules to use the new integration.

I request you to please review the setup and make sure that it is identical to your old integration. Once done, please process a new order and let us know if everything is working okay.

If you need any assistance, please feel free to reach out.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello, Touseef,



many thanks for adding FedEx Rest account.

I run a few tests in dermahealer.com and noticed several problems so far.



1) There is something wrong with the address formatting. Street name and number always printed twice.

- See label attached

- See screenshot of address correction window

This was never an issue with old API account



2) Automation rules do not work same way as in API

We have only 3 rules:

1 Fedex Intern Priority (default)

2 Fedex regional Economy

3 DHL

Now all new order are processed as DHL by default.



There are 2 unprocessed order that you can test freely.

Can you please check and fix those 2 issues?



Thanks

Arunas
comments.html_body:
Hello, Touseef,

many thanks for adding FedEx Rest account.
I run a few tests in dermahealer.com and noticed several problems so far.

1) There is something wrong with the address formatting. Street name and number always printed twice.
- See label attached
- See screenshot of address correction window
This was never an issue with old API account

2) Automation rules do not work same way as in API
We have only 3 rules:
1 Fedex Intern Priority (default)
2 Fedex regional Economy
3 DHL
Now all new order are processed as DHL by default.

There are 2 unprocessed order that you can test freely.
Can you please check and fix those 2 issues? 

Thanks
Arunas

  1) There is something wrong with the address formatting. Street name and number always printed twice.  - See label attached  - See screenshot of address correction window  This was never an issue with old API account  
  2) Automation rules do not work same way as in API  We have only 3 rules:

comments.body:
Hello Arunas,

Thank you for your response.

To answer your queries -
1. For this case, please allow us some time to check this case with our team. We will update you soon.
Regarding the address correction, you can ignore it as the address provided are generally authenticated by Shopify during checkout.

2. For the rules, the rules are configured to always use the cheapest available service for the label generation. No existing rule is available to make any service as default option for the orders.
As DHL service is the cheapest, the app is assigning the service to your orders.
If you have any particular criteria on which the services should be assigned (like Customer selected shipping option, order weight, etc), you can let us know and we will check the feasibility to apply the criteria in the rules.


Let us know if you have any further queries.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Arunas,
 
Thank you for your response.
 
To answer your queries - 
  1. For this case, please allow us some time to check this case with our team. We will update you soon.
    Regarding the address correction, you can ignore it as the address provided are generally authenticated by Shopify during checkout.
     
  2. For the rules, the rules are configured to always use the cheapest available service for the label generation. No existing rule is available to make any service as default option for the orders.
    As DHL service is the cheapest, the app is assigning the service to your orders.
    If you have any particular criteria on which the services should be assigned (like Customer selected shipping option, order weight, etc), you can let us know and we will check the feasibility to apply the criteria in the rules.
 
Let us know if you have any further queries.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1779378373815069?thread_ts=1778847898.801819&cid=C02E15X8X0C

Need assistance in understanding the logic with the Label Automation rule application when FedEx SOAP vs FedEx REST is enabled for the rules.
Customer has created 3 rules for his requirement -
1. Rule for FedEx with International Priority.
2. Rule for FedEx with Regional Economy.
3. Rule for DHL with Worldwide Express.


Customer is not using the app for rates.
When the FedEx SOAP is enabled in the rules, the orders are assigned FedEx International Priority upon import. If this service is not available, then FedEx Regional Economy is assigned. If both FedEx services are not available, then DHL is being assigned to the order by default.

This is the setup that the customer wants and is happy with.

Now, when REST is enabled in the FedEx rules, the cheapest is being assigned to the order, irrespective of the rule priority. This is NOT what customer wants.

I have attached a screen recording of the same for reference in the slack thread.

Please check and provide you input.

NOTE: Tested the Address Line twice case FedEx Regional Economy again and it is working as expected.

URL - dermahealer-com.myshopify.com
Account ID - 0cf46b3a-562f-410c-b9fd-786152020248
comments.html_body:
L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1779378373815069?thread_ts=1778847898.801819&cid=C02E15X8X0C
 
Need assistance in understanding the logic with the Label Automation rule application when FedEx SOAP vs FedEx REST is enabled for the rules.
Customer has created 3 rules for his requirement - 
  1. Rule for FedEx with International Priority.
  2. Rule for FedEx with Regional Economy.
  3. Rule for DHL with Worldwide Express.
 
Customer is not using the app for rates.
When the FedEx SOAP is enabled in the rules, the orders are assigned FedEx International Priority upon import. If this service is not available, then FedEx Regional Economy is assigned. If both FedEx services are not available, then DHL is being assigned to the order by default.
 
This is the setup that the customer wants and is happy with.
 
Now, when REST is enabled in the FedEx rules, the cheapest is being assigned to the order, irrespective of the rule priority. This is NOT what customer wants.
 
I have attached a screen recording of the same for reference in the slack thread.
 
Please check and provide you input.
 
NOTE: Tested the Address Line twice case FedEx Regional Economy again and it is working as expected.
 
URL - dermahealer-com.myshopify.com
Account ID - 0cf46b3a-562f-410c-b9fd-786152020248
